name.text: Why is the Human Development Index (HDI) considered a better measure of welfare ...

questiontext.text: Why is the Human Development Index (HDI) considered a better measure of welfare than GDP alone?

answer.text: It includes measures of health, education, and income.
It ignores economic growth entirely.
It only focuses on developed countries.
It does not consider inequality.

name.text: According to economic growth theory, why might poorer countries grow faster than...

questiontext.text: According to economic growth theory, why might poorer countries grow faster than richer ones?

answer.text: They have more advanced infrastructure.
They can adopt existing technologies more quickly.
They have higher savings rates.
They experience constant growth rates.

name.text: What is one of the main reasons why economists focus on real per capita GDP rath...

questiontext.text: What is one of the main reasons why economists focus on real per capita GDP rather than total GDP?

answer.text: It accounts for inflation and population growth.
It measures only consumer spending.
It provides a better measure of income inequality.
It excludes government spending.

name.text: What is a major factor contributing to differences in economic growth rates amon...

questiontext.text: What is a major factor contributing to differences in economic growth rates among countries?

answer.text: The average age of the population
The level of political and economic stability
The number of large corporations in the country
The availability of consumer goods

name.text: Why do some countries remain poor despite global economic growth?

questiontext.text: Why do some countries remain poor despite global economic growth?

answer.text: They lack access to trade and investment.
They prioritize economic growth over social programs.
Their populations prefer a lower standard of living.
They have fewer natural resources.
